entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The target crop for breeding drought tolerance, Arachis hypogaea L.

entities.name: POD YIELD
entities.description: The measure of productivity of the groundnut pods

entities.name: WATER-DEFICIT STRESS
entities.description: A condition of limited water availability causing plant stress

entities.name: WATER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which the plant utilizes water for growth

entities.name: WATER-LIMITED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental conditions where water is scarce

entities.name: SPAD CHLOROPHYLL METER READING
entities.description: A non-destructive method used to measure chlorophyll density and as a surrogate for water use efficiency

entities.name: SPECIFIC LEAF AREA
entities.description: A leaf trait associated with water use efficiency

entities.name: CHLOROPHYLL DENSITY
entities.description: The concentration of chlorophyll in the plant leaves

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: Stress experienced by plants due to lack of water

entities.name: RIL POPULATION
entities.description: A population of 280 recombinant inbred lines used for evaluation

entities.name: WELL-WATERED
entities.description: A control condition where plants receive optimal water

entities.name: YIELD PARAMETERS
entities.description: Various measurements used to assess the yield of the crop

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A continent where groundnut is primarily a rainfed crop

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: A continent where groundnut is primarily a rainfed crop

entities.name: RAINFED
entities.description: A farming condition where crops depend on rainfall

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A period of deficient rainfall leading to water stress

entities.name: POD YIELD PENALTY
entities.description: The reduction in pod yield under water-deficit stress compared to well-watered conditions

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics
